mnemonics: WHILE Looping: Work Happens In Looping Execution
keyPoints: A 'while' loop executes a block of code repeatedly as long as a given condition remains true
The condition is evaluated before the loop body is executed, making it a pre-test loop
It is crucial to have a statement inside the loop that eventually makes the condition false to avoid infinite loops
commonMistakes: Forgetting to update the loop control variable inside the loop body, leading to an infinite loop
Using an assignment operator (=) instead of an equality operator (==) in the loop condition
Placing a semicolon immediately after the while condition, which creates an empty loop body that may not execute as intended
